description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a dark brown, rugged surface and a somewhat flattened profile. One face of the stone features two distinct images: a photograph of a rustic building surrounded by trees and an iconographic portrait of a saint, both accompanied by Greek text. The stone is partially supported by a simple black metal frame, which appears to cradle it from below and behind, with some yellowish tape or ribbon attached to the frame. The surface of the stone is rough and uneven, with visible chips and natural texturing, suggesting it has been exposed to the elements. The overall appearance is that of a commemorative or decorative object, possibly used as a marker or monument in an urban or park setting. The combination of natural stone and human-made imagery gives it a unique, culturally significant presence.
description: A dark, rough stone with commemorative images and Greek text on one face, supported by a black metal frame with yellow tape.
description_view_0: Side view showing the stone's irregular, vertical profile and the supporting black metal frame with yellow tape.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the stone's broad, dark surface and the full extent of the supporting metal frame.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, emphasizing the stone's narrow edge and the frame's angular support structure.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the commemorative images and Greek text on the stone's main face.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature on a paved or landscaped surface
attribute_pattern: Artificial pattern with printed images and Greek text on one face; natural stone texture elsewhere with irregular mottling.
attribute_weathering: Surface is rough and chipped, indicating moderate weathering; images appear recently applied and less affected by aging.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with man-made surfaces due to its natural form, but the metal frame and imagery integrate it visually with urban commemorative elements.
